dialogos.texto_en: Ah, Fu-kun!
Nino and Itsuki, eh. What are you looking at?
Look, it's a Kariyushi shirt.\nDoesn't it look like something from Okinawa?
At first glance, I thought it was an Aloha shirt,\nbut it's something different, right?
I skimmed through the description here,\nand it seems like the origin is completely different.
There is also other trivia written here.\nFu-kun, you like this kind of stuff, don't you?
I don't mind learning something new.\nLet me see.
I see. Kariyushi shirts are formal attire,\nworn even in weddings in Okinawa.
So, they're also worn at official events.
There are designs for men and women,\nso why not make them matching souvenirs?
But, how should I put it,\nI wonder if this kind of patterned shirt would suit you,\nUesugi-kun...
My dad wears these kinds of clothes,\nand I don't think they look bad on him.
Hmm, I guess you'd look better\nin simple and neat clothes, Fu-kun.
Is that so?
(I have no idea on what basis they are judging my style.\n I don't think I can keep up with this conversation...)
So, let's avoid patterned cloth then%p-2;――
How about this calm-looking one?\nHere, Fu-kun, stand up straight.
Like this?
Yes, just like that.\nLet me put it on you like this%p-2;――
How is it? Does it suit me?
...It's kind of iffy.
Well, how should I put it,\nit makes you look quite mature...
(Is she saying I look old?)
We should look for something\nthat's not too flashy\nbut not too plain either.
Ah, how about this one?\nIt has a realistic goya pattern.
That's a bold design...
The pattern on this shirt is too lively for Fu-kun.
This shop has a lot of Kariyushi shirts.\nLet's try on more.
With this many options,\nwe'll definitely find the perfect one for Fu-kun!
...Hey, I thought we were looking for souvenirs?
Hmmm, this one is also iffy.\nBut it's better than the previous one, right?
Nino, what about this one?
Now, really.\nYou've been focusing too much on impact when choosing.\nPick something without patterns for once.
That's not true!\nRight, Uesugi-kun?!
Don't look at me for approval.\nI have no idea what your sense of style is.\nAnyway%p-2;――
Give me a break, would you?\nYou guys are clearly just playing around with me.
Hey, don't play dumb.
Oooh, come on.\nIt's not like we're losing anything.
I think we're losing time, though.
It'll become a fun memory.\nHey Fu-kun, try this one next!
None of these look good on me.\nI'm just a guy\nwho won't look good in Kariyushi shirts.
Huh? Are you sulking now?
I'm not sulking!
You're clearly sulking.\nYou have moments when you suddenly become childish. \nIt's cute.
D-don't say a guy is cute.\nThat's gross.
That's not true.\nRight now, you're really cute, Fu-kun.
I said stop it...
There are a lot of different Kariyushi shirts, huh.
I thought there were only short-sleeved shirts,\nbut I was surprised that there are long-sleeved shirts\nand polo shirts too.
I feel the same way.\nIt's fun to have a wider variety to choose from.
I can understand how you guys were having fun\nplaying with me like a toy.
Nevertheless, it's a little surprising.\nYou were actually paying attention.
What do you mean, 'paying attention'?
Well, I thought you weren't interested in clothes,\nso I assumed everything looked the same to you.
Come on, Itsuki...\nEven I'M not that indifferent, am I.
Phew, that was fun.\nFu-kun's Kariyushi shirt fashion show was a blast.
Yes, thanks to Uesugi-kun,\nwe were able to see\nthe hidden potential of Kariyushi shirts.
I was in a tough spot,\nhaving to try on shirt after shirt... But, well, it was a little fun.
That's good then.
By the way, let's not forget the purpose of\nus being here. Our souvenir search ain't over, right?
I know.\nI was seriously considering getting a shirt as a souvenir.
Those shops had a huge variety of patterns,\nif we search long enough,\nwe might find something that everyone likes.
It seems like there's some room for flexibility,\nlike using the same patterns but with different base colors.
Yeah, that's right.\nLet's include Kariyushi shirts\nin our list of candidates for suitable souvenirs.
